African Swine Fever is spreading in parts of Europe, Asia, and Africa. The risk of introduction to Great Britain is being highly monitored due to widespread presence in Europe. Over 232 cases in wild boar reported across 10 municipalities in Catalonia, Spain, by March 19, 2026. While wild boar cases are high, commercial pig farms have remained largely unaffected, though high-risk restrictions are in place.
In Europe, the disease continues to spread, with new detections of ASF in wild boar in Saxony, Germany, and outbreaks in domestic pigs in Moldova, Romania, Serbia, and Ukraine.

(Strain type) HPAI H5N1 is the hazard of interest, other HPAI H5 strains (mainly H5N8) when no relevant information is available for H5N1. 
(Transmission) Two major routes of exposure and infection are considered: direct exposure via direct contact with infected carcasses and indirect exposure via contaminated environments or fomites.
In Apr 2026, HPAI H5N1 was confirmed in commercial poultry in Lincolnshire, England.
